---
title: Memory Adapter
description: In-process, volatile cache for development and tests.
icon: Cpu
---

## When to use

Use the memory adapter during development and in tests. It stores entries in a JavaScript `Map`, requires no external infrastructure, and is reset when the process exits.

**Do not use** in production if you need persistence or shared state across multiple instances.

## Installation

```bash
bun add @justwant/cache
```

No additional packages required.

## Setup

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { memoryAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/memory";

export const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
});
```

## Options

`memoryAdapter()` takes no options.

## Behavior

- TTL is enforced via `Date.now()` comparison on read — no background timer.
- `clear()` removes all entries.
- `keys()` returns all non-expired keys.
- `invalidateTag()` is emulated via an internal reverse-index.
